blocks.content: Back corner. Not isolated, not obvious. That's what I told myself.
A kid sat down across from me without asking. Just — sat. Like we'd been assigned to each other.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: I've been trying to figure out the social map of this place since 8am. It's like they already did orientation without us.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: Yeah but still. Even the freshmen who've been coming up from the middle school — they already know where they land.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: How long have you been analyzing the cafeteria?

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: Since I walked in. I do this everywhere. Can't help it.

blocks.speaker: DeShawn
blocks.content: Cause he's been scanning this side of the cafeteria for the last five minutes. Very subtle. But not subtle enough.
I looked up. Jonathan was mid-conversation at the center table — but his eyes were doing a slow sweep of the room. They landed on me.

blocks.prompt: Jonathan's looking. DeShawn's watching you look at Jonathan.

options.label: Give him a nod. You see him.
